taxonomy: Rapuntium gibbosum, commonly known as the Giant Hogweed, is classified within the family Apiaceae. This family is a large and diverse group of plants commonly known as the carrot or parsley family. The genus Rapuntium is sometimes considered to be synonymous with Heracleum, but when considering species like this one, it is placed in the genus Heracleum. Heracleum is a genus of large, umbelliferous flowering plants in the family Apiaceae. The Giant Hogweed is native to Europe and Western Asia.
uses: Rapuntium gibbosum, also known as the Globe Thistle, has been used in traditional medicine for its potential anti-inflammatory properties.
